Emergency Autoland.clue: Total servo count in a Garmin Emergency Autoland system.
Emergency Autoland.explanation: Garmin Autoland uses four total servos.

Emergency Autoland.clue: These two events can start an Emergency Autoland sequence.
Emergency Autoland.explanation: EAL starts on pilot inactivity detection or when the Autoland button is pressed manually.

Emergency Autoland.clue: Autoland weighs these four factors when choosing a landing airport.
Emergency Autoland.explanation: The system considers traffic, weather, fuel, and landing distance.

Emergency Autoland.clue: This panel configures the aircraft systems during an Autoland event.
Emergency Autoland.explanation: The relay interface panel orchestrates aircraft system setup.

Emergency Autoland.clue: EAL brake servos stop the aircraft by driving these hydraulic components.
Emergency Autoland.explanation: Brake servos mechanically drive the hydraulic brake master cylinders.

Emergency Autoland.clue: After stopping on the runway, Autoland does this next.
choices: Taxis off the runway then stops
Emergency Autoland.explanation: The final action is engine shutdown on the runway.

Emergency Autoland.clue: EAL configures this full set of systems to prepare for autonomous landing.
Emergency Autoland.explanation: EAL sets up the full landing configuration: gear, flaps, deice, firewall shutoff, and air data.

Emergency Autoland.clue: Location of the EAL throttle servos — mechanically tied to the throttle levers.
Emergency Autoland.explanation: Throttle servos mount in the pedestal and drive the existing throttle levers.

Emergency Autoland.clue: The required Garmin avionics platform for Emergency Autoland.
Emergency Autoland.explanation: EAL is certified on G1000 NXi flight deck with GFC 700 autopilot.

Emergency Autoland.clue: This is when a pilot may use Emergency Autoland.
Emergency Autoland.explanation: EAL is approved for genuine emergency use only.

Emergency Autoland.clue: EAL announces the emergency via these two channels.
Emergency Autoland.explanation: EAL speaks to ATC and simultaneously sets the emergency transponder code.
